title: Monoon liukiuense
intro: Monoon liukiuense, also known as the Lithuanian dogwood, is a plant species belonging to the Annonaceae family, characterized by its small, white flowers and edible fruit. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Monoon liukiuense include annonaceous acetogenins, such as annonacin and corossolol, which have been shown to exhibit anticancer, antiparasitic, and antifungal activities. In traditional medicine, Monoon liukiuense is often made into herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues.
